title: Power converter with modular stages connected by floating terminals
abstract: An apparatus for electric power conversion includes a converter having a regulating circuit and switching network. The regulating circuit has magnetic storage elements, and switches connected to the magnetic storage elements and controllable to switch between switching configurations. The regulating circuit maintains an average DC current through a magnetic storage element. The switching network includes charge storage elements connected to switches that are controllable to switch between plural switch configurations. In one configuration, the switches forms an arrangement of charge storage elements in which at least one charge storage element is charged using the magnetic storage element through the network input or output port. In another, the switches form an arrangement of charge storage elements in which an element discharges using the magnetic storage element through one of the input port and output port of the switching network.
claims: 1. A power converter comprising: a plurality of switches configured to be connected to a plurality of capacitors to form a first switched-capacitor converter and configured to switch between a plurality of switching configurations to charge or discharge the plurality of capacitors such that the first switched-capacitor converter transforms a first voltage into a second voltage, wherein the first switched-capacitor converter is configured to couple to one or more switched-capacitor converters, wherein the plurality of switches comprises a first set of switches and a second set of switches, wherein, in a first switching configuration of the plurality of switching configurations, the first set of switches are configured to be turned on and the second set of switches are configured to be turned off, and wherein, in a second switching configuration of the plurality of switching configurations, the first set of switches are configured to be turned off and the second set of switches are configured to be turned on, wherein each switch of the second set of switches is coupled to at least one switch of the first set of switches, wherein, for each switch of the second set of switches, a node between the switch of the second set of switches and the at least one switch of the first set of switches coupled to the switch of the second set of switches is configured to be connected to a corresponding one of the plurality of capacitors, and wherein, for each switch of a first subset of the second set of switches, the switch of the first subset and the at least one switch of the first set of switches coupled to the switch of the first subset are coupled between a pair of nodes configured to be coupled to a regulating circuit.
